questionText: What methods do you use to drive investment in emissions reduction activities?
questionKo: 배출량 감축활동에 대한 투자를 유도하기 위해 사용한 방법을 입력하십시오.
sectorTags: Financial services, Oil & Gas, Coal, General, Aviation, Capital goods, Transport OEMS - EPM, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Agricultural commodities, Transport OEMS, Energy utilities & power generators, Cement, Food, beverage & tobacco, Paper & forestry, Real estate, Construction
simpleHelp: This question provides data users with more transparency into your organization's approach to realizing emissions reductions and progress towards targets.

options: Dedicated budget for low-carbon product R&D
Dedicated budget for other emissions reduction activities
Partnering with governments on technology development
optionsKo: Dedicated budget for low-carbon product R&D
Dedicated budget for other emissions reduction activities
Partnering with governments on technology development

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Yes" in response to column 1 "Emissions reduction initiatives that were active within the reporting year" in 7.55
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Yes" in response to 열 1 "Emissions reduction 이니셔티브 that were active within the 보고연도" in 7.55

guidance.text:
General 
This question is intended to gather information on the ways in which capital is directed towards emissions reduction activities within your organization's value chain (i.e. Scope 1, Scope 2 and/or Scope 3), and/or the way in which initiatives are identified. If your organization uses an internal carbon price you are encouraged to report this here in addition to in module 5 "Business strategy" section "Pricing Environmental Externalities".
Method 
(column 1) 
Select the types of methods that you employ to help to channel funds towards emissions reduction initiatives.
Comment (column 2) (optional) 
Provide additional details or examples as necessary.
guidance.textKo: 일반 This 문항 is intended to gather information on the ways in which capital is directed towards emissions reduction activities within 귀사의 가치사슬 (i.e. Scope 1, Scope 2 및/또는 Scope 3), 및/또는 the way in which 이니셔티브 are identified. 만약 귀사 uses an 내부 탄소가격 you are encouraged to report this here in addition to in module 5 "사업전략" section "Pricing Environmental Externalities". 방법 (열 1) Select the types of 방법s that you employ to help to channel funds towards emissions reduction 이니셔티브. 비고 (열 2) (optional) Provide additional details 또는 examples as necessary.

guidance.text:
Marginal Abatement Cost Curves 
Marginal Abatement Cost Curves, or MACCs, provide a method of evaluating potential emissions reduction activities. They provide a visual comparison of the marginal abatement costs for different projects.
MACCs can be generated to evaluate options at any level of organization - from individual business divisions, to the overall business and to sectors and countries/areas, evaluating individual projects, programs or policies.
Marginal abatement costs are calculated by dividing the costs of the project (calculated from the initial cost minus any savings made as a result of the project) by the greenhouse gas emissions saved over a specified investment timeframe.
These are then arranged with the lowest costs (sometime negative cost) on the left, increasing in cost to the right, creating the curve. An example taken from McKinsey & Company " Impact of the financial crisis on carbon economics: Version 2.1 of the global greenhouse gas abatement cost curve " 
Those projects/initiatives where there are cost savings to be made over the lifetime of the project as a result of the emissions savings made, and therefore, even without a commitment to carbon reduction investment, should be implemented from a cost saving point of view. Where positive costs are associated with the proposals the MACC curve can be used to suggest the lowest cost options for achieving a particular target ( McKinsey & Company, 2010 , page 8).
As with all evaluation methods, the accuracy of the MACC will depend on that of the input data.
guidance.textKo: Marginal Abatement Cost Curves Marginal Abatement Cost Curves, 또는 MACCs, provide a 방법 of evaluating potential emissions reduction activities. They provide a visual comparison 의 marginal abatement costs for different projects. MACCs can be generated to evaluate options at 하나 이상 level of organization - from individual business divisions, to the overall business 및 to sectors 및 countries/areas, evaluating individual projects, programs 또는 policies. Marginal abatement costs are calculated by dividing the costs 의 project (calculated from the initial cost minus 하나 이상 savings made as a result 의 project) by the 온실가스 배출량 saved over a specified investment timeframe. These are then arranged with the lowest costs (sometime negative cost) on the left, increasing in cost to the right, creating the curve. An example taken from McKinsey & Company " Impact 의 financial crisis on carbon economics: Version 2.1 의 global greenhouse gas abatement cost curve " Those projects/이니셔티브 여기서 there are cost savings to be made over the lifetime 의 project as a result 의 emissions savings made, 및 therefore, even without a commitment to carbon reduction investment, 해야 합니다 be implemented from a cost saving 점 of view. 여기서 positive costs are associated with the proposals the MACC curve can be used to suggest the lowest cost options for achieving a particular 목표 ( McKinsey & Company, 2010 , page 8). As with all evaluation 방법s, the accuracy 의 MACC will depend on that 의 input data.

change.question_ko: 배출량 감축활동 투자를 이끌어내기 위해 사용한 방법을 입력해 주십시오 . 이 질문은 M7.55 에 대한 응답으로 " 예 " 를 선택한 경우에만 나타납니다 . ` M7. Environmental Performance - Climate Change 방법 관련 설명 규제 요건 / 표준 준수 에너지 효율을 위한 전용 예산 저탄소 제품 R&D 를 위한 전용 예산 기타 배출량 감축 활동을 위한 전용 예산 직원 참여 재무 최적화 계산 탄소 내부 가격 내부 인센티브 / 인정 프로그램 내부 금융 메커니즘 낮은 투자 수익률 (ROI) 사양 한계 감축 비용 곡선 기술 개발을 위한 정부와의 파트너십 기타 최대 1,500 자로 작성
change.q2025_text: What methods do you use to drive investment in emissions reduction activities?
change.change_summary: Added guidance to specify only initiatives to reduce emissions in the organization's value chain are relevant.
